content.text:
{
  "warning": [
    ""requests[0].chunk" must be greater than or equal to 1; using 1"
  ],
  "pages": [
    {
      "total_chunks": 1,
      "content": "{
  "question": {
    "body_markdown": "I am trying to create an application in visual studio that will be able to access a .dll file that already exists. I need the application to call up routines. I also have a header file that already exists.\r\n\r\nI have been researching on the internet and have found that I need to create a .lib file. Looking at similar questions on here I found a link: http://support.microsoft.com/kb/131313\r\nI cannot however follow the directions.\r\n\r\nThe information in the link says to make a DEF file ( I read elsewhere that this needs to be compiled as a DLL with the same name, but not sure what that name is, the same name as the .dll file?). But I do not understand the first direction, to 'Use DUMPBIN /EXPORTS'. I then need to 'stub out' functions, and then something to do with .OBJ files (I do not know what these files are).\r\n\r\nAre there any step-by-step directions, similar to the link above, that are easy to follow?",
    "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file",
    "title": "How to make a .lib file when have a .dll file and a header file",
    "score": 73,
    "tags": [
      "c++",
      "dll",
      "header",
      "function"
    ],
    "creation_date": 1329736806,
    "is_answered": true,
    "last_activity_date": 1781606738,
    "answer_count": 7,
    "question_id": 9360280
  },
  "answers": [
    {
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0",
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "is_accepted": false,
      "answer_id": 9360485,
      "score": 23,
      "last_activity_date": 1708984879,
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 690,
        "user_id": 311055,
        "display_name": "Desu_Never_Lies",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/311055/desu-never-lies"
      },
      "body_markdown": "You can use Digital Mars's [IMPLIB][1] tool. It can create a lib file using only the dll, without any need for a .def file.\r\n\r\nYou can download it at https://ftp.digitalmars.com/bup.zip.\r\n\r\nThe command line is:\r\n\r\n    implib.exe /s mydll.lib mydll.dll\r\n\r\n  [1]: http://digitalmars.com/ctg/implib.html",
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/9360485#9360485",
      "last_edit_date": 1708984879,
      "creation_date": 1329737814
    },
    {
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/16127548#16127548",
      "last_edit_date": 1559092762,
      "answer_id": 16127548,
      "creation_date": 1366513777,
      "is_accepted": false,
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 1915,
        "user_id": 1778714,
        "display_name": "Sany Liew",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/1778714/sany-liew"
      },
      "score": 103,
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0",
      "last_activity_date": 1559092762,
      "body_markdown": "You're going to need [Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Express][1] (or any other source of MSVC command line tools), and your DLL.\r\n\r\n  [1]: http://www.microsoft.com/visualstudio/eng/downloads\r\n\r\nSteps:\r\n\r\n 1. `dumpbin /EXPORTS yourfile.dll > yourfile.exports`\r\n 2. Paste the names of the needed functions from `yourfile.exports` into a new `yourfile.def` file. Add a line with the word `EXPORTS` at the top of this file.\r\n 3. Run the following commands from `VC\\bin` directory (the one where `lib.exe` and other compile tools reside).\r\n\r\n \r\n\r\n     vcvars32.bat\r\n\r\n     lib /def:yourfile.def /out:yourfile.lib\r\n\r\nor for x64 builds\r\n\r\n     lib /def:yourfile.def /machine:x64 /out:yourfile64.lib\r\n\r\n\r\nYou should get two files generated: `yourfile.lib` and `yourfile.exp`\r\n"
    },
    {
      "creation_date": 1577997782,
      "answer_id": 59569755,
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "last_activity_date": 1577997782,
      "score": -1,
      "body_markdown": "I might have the answer. I did this when I was creating a .exe console application that needed a .dll file. I ran into the problem as well. When I tried the IMPLIB application, it couldn't find any export files. You need to add an ```#ifdef FILENAME_EXPORTS``` (replace FILENAME with your .dll file name) and create an ```_API```. Here is the code for the ```#ifdef``` export api commands:\r\n```\r\n#ifdef FILENAME_EXPORTS\r\n#define FILENAME_API __declspec(dllexport)\r\n#else\r\n#define FILENAME_API __declspec(dllimport)\r\n#endif\r\n```\r\nNow that you have the Export API defined, you need to apply it to all the functions in your header file in the .dll project. For example:\r\n```\r\nvoid FILENAME_API function();\r\n```\r\nDeclare your export functions normally, but include the API between the declarer type and the function name.\r\n\r\nFor defining the function in the .cpp file in the .dll project, you don't need the API in the declaration.\r\n\r\nHere is an example of *filename*.h and *filename.cpp* with all the code.\r\n```\r\n// Code for filename.h\r\n#pragma once\r\n\r\n// Define your Export API\r\n#ifdef FILENAME_EXPORTS\r\n#define FILENAME_API __declspec(dllexport)\r\n#else\r\n#define FILENAME_API __declspec(dllimport)\r\n#endif\r\n\r\n// Declare your functions with your API\r\n\r\nvoid FILENAME_API function1();\r\nvoid FILENAME_API function2();\r\nvoid FILENAME_API function3();\r\n\r\n-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\r\n// Code for filename.cpp\r\n#include <iostream>\r\n#include \"pch.h\"\r\n#include \"filename.h\"\r\nusing namespace std;\r\n\r\nvoid function1()\r\n{\r\n    cout << \"Hello Function1!\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nvoid function2()\r\n{\r\n    cout << \"Hello Function2!\";\r\n}\r\n\r\nvoid function3()\r\n{\r\n    cout << \"Hello Function3!\";\r\n}\r\n```\r\nNow when you compile the project, you should see the .dll, .lib, and .exp files in the folder where the compiled files are saved to. Now you can link the .exe file with the .lib file. You're Welcome!",
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 19,
        "user_id": 12090607,
        "display_name": "ScotthomasPrime",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/12090607/scotthomasprime"
      },
      "is_accepted": false,
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/59569755#59569755",
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0"
    },
    {
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0",
      "last_edit_date": 1632380047,
      "creation_date": 1632368349,
      "answer_id": 69293718,
      "body_markdown": "There is a much simpler way to create a `.def` file. Search the web for the `gendef utility` (you may already have it if you have `Mingw-64`). Once you have it, just go to a command line and type,\r\n```\r\ngendef myfile.dll\r\n```\r\nAnd it will create a `myfile.def`. After that, just use `lib.exe` to create the `myfile.lib` file as explained by John.",
      "is_accepted": false,
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 69,
        "user_id": 15880739,
        "display_name": "G. Adam Stanislav",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/15880739/g-adam-stanislav"
      },
      "score": 7,
      "last_activity_date": 1632380047,
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/69293718#69293718"
    },
    {
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0",
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/71100373#71100373",
      "last_activity_date": 1644753204,
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "creation_date": 1644753204,
      "is_accepted": false,
      "score": -1,
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 1,
        "user_id": 18195369,
        "display_name": "Ahmed Osama",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/18195369/ahmed-osama"
      },
      "answer_id": 71100373,
      "body_markdown": "First type \r\n\r\n#define YOURPROJECT_API _declspec(dllexport)\r\n\r\nvoid yourf()\r\n\r\ncpp\r\n\r\n    #include \"pch.h\"\r\n    #include \"YOURPROJECT.H\"\r\n    void yourf() {}\r\n\r\nthen include them\r\n\r\nlinker->input->def file then inhert from parent or project default\r\n\r\ncompile\r\n\r\n"
    },
    {
      "last_activity_date": 1650104157,
      "body_markdown": "Here is a solution using [Cygwin][1]:\r\n - install `gendef` and `mingw64-x86_64-binutils` Cygwin packages\r\n - run in Cygwin console:\r\n   ```\r\n   gendef.exe awesome.dll\r\n   x86_64-w64-mingw32-dlltool.exe -d awesome.def -l awesome.lib\r\n   ```\r\n - you get `awesome.lib` in the same dir.\r\n\r\nVS 2019 linker linked just fine using this file.\r\n\r\n\r\n  [1]: https://www.cygwin.com/",
      "is_accepted": false,
      "answer_id": 71892981,
      "score": 4,
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0",
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 471,
        "user_id": 2745509,
        "display_name": "user2745509",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/2745509/user2745509"
      },
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/71892981#71892981",
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "creation_date": 1650104157
    },
    {
      "owner": {
        "reputation": 1222,
        "user_id": 7141410,
        "display_name": "jtxkopt - STOP GENOCIDE",
        "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/users/7141410/jtxkopt-stop-genocide"
      },
      "link": "https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9360280/how-to-make-a-lib-file-when-have-a-dll-file-and-a-header-file/79960588#79960588",
      "score": 0,
      "answer_id": 79960588,
      "body_markdown": "For those looking for an automated way to create such import libraries, here is another tool called `LibGen` that I created several years ago: [LibGen](https://github.com/semihartan/LibGen).\r\n\r\nJust call it like that:\r\n\r\n```\r\nlibgen sample.dll\r\n```",
      "is_accepted": false,
      "creation_date": 1781606738,
      "question_id": 9360280,
      "last_activity_date": 1781606738,
      "content_license": "CC BY-SA 4.0"
    }
  ]
}",
      "chunk": 1
    }
  ]
}

warning: "requests[0].chunk" must be greater than or equal to 1; using 1
